title: lachemilla pascuorum
intro: Lachemilla pascuorum, also known as the sticky mouse-ear or sticky cinquefoil, is a low-growing perennial herb in the rose family, Rosaceae. Native to Europe and Asia, it has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as soothing skin irritations and wounds, reducing fever, and aiding in digestion. Lachemilla pascuorum can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its leaves and flowers are often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
